# LearnRAG

LearnRAG is the internet's most beginner-friendly, hands-on platform for learning
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). Free forever. No signup required. No paywall.
Built for engineers and CS students who want to build real things, not just read theory.

## What is RAG?

RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) combines a search step with an LLM generation step.
Instead of asking the model to rely on its training data, you first retrieve relevant
documents from your own knowledge base, then ask the LLM to answer using only those
documents. This eliminates hallucinations and keeps answers grounded in facts.

The canonical reference: Lewis et al. (2020), "Retrieval-Augmented Generation for
Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks" (NeurIPS 2020). https://arxiv.org/abs/2005.11401

## Curriculum — 8 Chapters

- Chapter 1: Why RAG Exists — /learn/1-why-rag/
  The memory cutoff problem, hallucinations, RAG vs fine-tuning, when to use each.

- Chapter 2: Data Ingestion & Chunking — /learn/2-chunking/
  Context window limits, fixed-size vs sentence vs sliding window vs semantic chunking,
  chunk overlap, metadata attachment.

- Chapter 3: Embeddings & The Vector Space — /learn/3-embeddings/
  What embeddings are, cosine similarity, embedding model selection, the critical
  rule of model consistency between indexing and querying.

- Chapter 4: Vector Databases — /learn/4-vector-databases/
  Why SQL fails at similarity search, HNSW indexing, ChromaDB vs Qdrant vs FAISS,
  persistent vs in-memory storage.

- Chapter 5: Retrieval Strategies — /learn/5-retrieval/
  Naive semantic search, hybrid search (vector + BM25), MMR, cross-encoder re-ranking,
  metadata filtering.

- Chapter 6: The Prompt Layer — /learn/6-prompting/
  RAG prompt anatomy, lost-in-the-middle problem, context window math, citation prompting,
  prompt templates for Q&A, summarisation, and conversational use cases.

- Chapter 7: Evaluation — /learn/7-evaluation/
  Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Context Precision, Context Recall metrics. RAGAS
  framework. Building a test set. The improvement loop.

- Chapter 8: Advanced RAG Patterns — /learn/8-advanced-patterns/
  HyDE, Query Decomposition, Self-RAG, Corrective RAG (CRAG), Multi-Vector Retrieval,
  Agentic RAG.

## Interactive Tools

- Playground (in-browser RAG pipeline, no API key needed): /playground/
  Ingest documents, chunk them, embed with Transformers.js, run similarity search,
  and generate answers — all in the browser.

- Vector Database Picker (4-question decision tool): /library/database-picker/

- Embedding Model Leaderboard: /library/model-leaderboard/

## Hands-On Labs

- Lab 1: LangChain + ChromaDB — /labs/1-langchain-chromadb/
- Lab 2: LlamaIndex Comparison — /labs/2-llamaindex-comparison/
- Lab 3: Add a Re-ranker — /labs/3-add-a-reranker/
- Lab 4: Evaluate with RAGAS — /labs/4-evaluate-with-ragas/
- Lab 5: Deploy to Hugging Face Spaces — /labs/5-deploy-to-huggingface/

## Badge System

Progress is tracked through four badges stored in localStorage (no account needed):
- Explorer: Complete Ch 1–2 + run 1 query in Playground
- Builder: Complete Ch 3–5 + finish Lab 1
- Engineer: Complete Ch 6–7 + finish Lab 3
- Architect: Complete Ch 8 + finish Lab 5

## Technical Stack

Built with Astro + Starlight. In-browser AI via Transformers.js (no server costs).
Diagrams via Mermaid.js. Static search via Pagefind. Hosted on Vercel.

## Source Policy

All factual claims are sourced from Tier 1 sources: original research papers (Lewis 2020,
RAGAS paper by Shahul Es et al. 2023, HNSW paper by Malkov & Yashunin 2018, HyDE by Gao
et al. 2022, Self-RAG by Asai et al. 2023, CRAG by Yan et al. 2024), official library
documentation (LangChain, LlamaIndex, ChromaDB, Qdrant, Transformers.js), and the
Hugging Face MTEB Leaderboard for benchmark data.
